zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Daniel II於1350年出生在英國爾時，他的父親Thomas Daniel25歲，母親Katherine de Tabley31歲。他於1379年與Elizabeth Aston在英國爾結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1432年在英國爾塔波享年82歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Daniel II于1350年出生在英国爾时，他的父亲Thomas Daniel25岁，母亲Katherine de Tabley31岁。他于1379年与Elizabeth Aston在英国爾结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1432年在英国爾塔波享年82岁。

nameMeanings.name: Daniel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Spanish, Portuguese, German, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Romanian, Jewish, Assyrian/Chaldean, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania); Breton (rarely Le Daniel); Hungarian (Dániel): from the Biblical personal name Daniel, Syriac (Assyrian/Chaldean) Dānīʾēl, Hungarian Dániel (from Hebrew Daniyyel ‘God is my judge’), borne by a major prophet in the Bible. The chief factor influencing the popularity of the personal name (and hence the frequency of the surname) was undoubtedly the dramatic story in the Book of Daniel recounting the prophet's steadfast adherence to his religious faith despite pressure and persecution from the Mesopotamian kings in whose court he served: Nebuchadnezzar, Belshazzar, and Darius. The name was also borne by a 2nd-century Christian martyr and by a 9th-century hermit, the legend of whose lives were popular among Christians during the Middle Ages. Among Orthodox Christians in Eastern Europe the name was also popular as being that of a 4th-century Persian martyr, who was venerated in the Orthodox Church. In France, this surname is most common in Brittany.

Irish (Tipperary and Waterford): shortened form of McDaniel , which is actually a variant of McDonnell , from the Gaelic form of Irish Donal (equivalent to Scottish Donald), erroneously associated with the Biblical personal name Daniel. See also O'Donnell .
Americanized or Germanized form of Slovenian Danijel: from the Biblical personal name Danijel ‘Daniel’ (see 1 above).

variants.name: Daane
meanings: Dutch: from a short form of the personal name   Daniel  . Compare   Dane  .

variants.name: Dan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Ha To, Hieu, Lam, Chinh, Dung, Dung Anh, Long, Ly, Mai, Minh, Nga, Nguyen Van.  Romanian Corneliu, Silviu, Constantin, Dumitru, Ilie, Rodica, Stelian, Vasile.
Romanian; Hungarian (mainly Dán): from a short form of the Biblical personal name Daniel (Hungarian Dániel).
Romanian: from a short form of the Slavic personal names   Bogdan   and   Prodan  .

variants.name: Dacko
meanings: Polish: from a pet form of an Old Polish compound personal names beginning with Da- (for example, Dalebor), or of the Biblical names Daniel or Dawid (Polish form of David ).
Americanized form of Ukrainian Datsko: from a pet form of the personal name Danylo, Ukrainian equivalent of Daniel .

variants.name: McDaniel
meanings: Irish: altered form of McDonnell , itself an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Domhnaill ‘son of Donal’, from association of the Gaelic patronymic with the Biblical personal name Daniel .

variants.name: Danel
meanings: French and Polish: from a variant of the personal name   Daniel  .

variants.name: Danca
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Nicola, Salvatore.
Portuguese (Dança): perhaps a nickname from dança ‘dance’.
Italian (D'Anca): probably a nickname from the vocabulary word anca ‘hip, haunch’.

variants.name: Dani
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Narendra, Saryu, Shashi, Bachu, Chandu, Devyani, Dilip, Dinesh, Hasit, Kaushik, Kirit, Manisha.
Italian, Romanian, and German; Slovak (also Dáni): from a short form of the personal name Daniel (Italian Daniele).
Albanian: from Dan (definite form Dani), a short form of the Muslim personal name Ramadan .

variants.name: Dagneau
meanings: French: variant, written under the influence of the word agneau ‘lamb’, of Daniau, which is from the personal name Daniau, a variant of Daniel . Compare Daignault , Daigneau , Daigneault , and Danyow .

History: The progenitor of the (majority of the) American Dagneaus was Michel Dagneau, sieur de Douville from Deux-Jumeaux in Calvados, France, who married Marie Lamy in Sorel, QC, in 1688. His descendants mostly bear altered forms of the surname, mentioned above.

variants.name: Dangel
meanings: German: from a vernacular form of the personal name   Daniel  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace).

variants.name: Danek
meanings: Czech (Daněk), Slovak, and Polish: from a pet form of Daniel or of any of the Slavic compound personal names formed with the element dan ‘given’ (see Dana ).
Czech (Daněk): nickname from daněk ‘fallow deer’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Daniel II was born in 1350, in Cheshire, England, his father, Thomas Daniel, was 25 and his mother, Katherine de Tabley, was 31. He married Elizabeth Aston in 1379, in Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1432, in Tabley, Cheshire, England, at the age of 82.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Daniel II est né en 1350, à Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Daniel, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Katherine de Tabley, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Aston en 1379, à Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1432, à Tabley, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 82 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Daniel II 1350 in Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Daniel 25 und seine Mutter Katherine de Tabley 31. Er hat 1379 in Cheshire, England Elizabeth Aston geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1432 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Tabley, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Daniel II è nato il nel 1350 nel Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas Daniel, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Katherine de Tabley, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Aston nel 1379, nel Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1432, nel Tabley, Cheshire, England, all'età di 82 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Daniel IIが1350年にイギリスチェシュアイアで生まれた時，父Thomas Danielは25歳，母Katherine de Tableyは31歳であった。1379年にイギリスチェシュアイアでElizabeth Astonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1432年にイギリスチェシュアイアタブリーで82歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Daniel II(이)가 1350년 영국 체실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Daniel(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine de Tabley(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1379년 영국 체실르에서 Elizabeth Aston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1432년 영국 체실르 타블리에서 82세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Daniel II nasceu em 1350, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Daniel, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Katherine de Tabley, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Aston em 1379, em Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1432, em Tabley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 82 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Daniel II родился в 1350 г. в местности чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Daniel было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine de Tabley, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Aston в 1379 г. в местности чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1432 г. в местности табли, чешир, Англия в возрасте 82 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Daniel II nació en 1350, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Daniel, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Katherine de Tabley, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Aston en 1379, en Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1432, en Tabley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 82 años.
